A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker, boosting Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC, the world's biggest contract chipmaker. ::Archive ::AMD For Apple, the deal would help diversify its manufacturing base and add chip capacity at a time when TSMC, its key partner, has been stretched thin by surging demand from AI chip-making clients such as Nvidia and AMD. Trump's social media post about the agreement did not specify which chips Intel would make for Apple. The president said it was his latest effort to help Intel, in which the U.S. government has a 10% stake. ::Archive The Trump administration has been stepping up efforts to secure U.S. supply chains for semiconductors and critical minerals, including taking equity stakes in companies to reduce reliance on China. Apple and Intel did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.
